person.name: Matilda

birthPlace.address: Courtenay, Departement du Loiret, Centre, France

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Per "Medieval Lands, 2nd Edition": "Two versions of her parentage are recorded. Father is either GEOFFROY de Crimes or GUILLAUME de Curcy; mother is Mathilde d'Avranches. Bracton records a claim, dated 1222, by "Matillis de Courteney" against "Robert de Curtenay" concerning "the manor of Ocumptona", which records that "Robert" claimed that the land was "the inheritance of Matillidis de Aueregnes" who had "two daughters...Hawisiam his first-born mother [...the daughter of Geofrid de Crimes of the first husband of Matillidis de Auerenches] et… Matillidem". However, another claim recorded by Bracton, also dated 1222, by "Matillis de Curtenay" against "Robert de Curtenay" concerning "the manor of Chamelegha" states that "Robertus filius Regis...Matilides de Auerenches his wife" held the land which was inherited by "Hawisie daughter of his mother of the same Robert de Curtenay who was the daughter of William de Curcy the husband of the same Matillides".

She was buried at Forde Abbey in Dorset.

zh.lifeSummary: Matilda是William de Courcy和Matilda d'Avranches的女兒，於大約1150年出生在法國崔-瓦爾 德 洛易洛易特庫尼。她和Reinauld De Seigneur De Courtenay至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1219年7月31日在英國戴文享年70歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾福特 布里奇。

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda was born about 1150, in Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France as the daughter of William de Courcy and Matilda d'Avranches. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Reinauld De Seigneur De Courtenay. She died on 31 July 1219, in Devon, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in Ford Bridge, Herefordshire, England.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Reinauld De Seigneur De Courtenay. Elle est décédée le 31 juillet 1219, à Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Ford Bridge, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

Sie hatte mit Reinauld De Seigneur De Courtenay mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 31. ja.lifeSummary: Matildaは約1150年にフランスセントレ-ヴァル ド ロイアーロイアーエトコウルテナイでWilliam de CourcyとMatilda d'Avranchesの娘として生まれた。彼女はReinauld De Seigneur De Courtenayとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1219年7月31日にイギリスデボンで70歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアフォード ブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.Courtenay, Departement du Loiret, Centre: 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 쿠르텐아이
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda(은)는 대략 1150년 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 쿠르텐아이에서 William de Courcy(와)과 Matilda d'Avranches의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Reinauld De Seigneur De Courtenay(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1219년 7월 31일 영국 데폰에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 포드 브리지에 묻혔다.

Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Reinauld De Seigneur De Courtenay. ru.Courtenay, Departement du Loiret, Centre: коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Matilda родилась около 1150 г. в местности коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция. Её родителей звали William de Courcy и Matilda d'Avranches. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Reinauld De Seigneur De Courtenay. Она умерла 31 июля 1219 г. в местности девон, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности форд бридж, хирефордшир, Англия.
